mnemonics.title: Stack LIFO
mnemonics.content: **L**ast **I**n **F**irst **O**ut — Think of a stack of plates: the last plate placed on top is the first one you remove.

mnemonics.title: Queue FIFO
mnemonics.content: **F**irst **I**n **F**irst **O**ut — Think of a real-world line: whoever waits in line first gets served first.

mnemonics.title: Queue Pointers
mnemonics.content: **E**nter at **R**ear, **E**xit at **F**ront — Remember: Rear (back) = Enqueue, Front = Dequeue. Items enter from the back and leave from the front like a pipe.

mnemonics.title: Pop/Dequeue Memory Steps
mnemonics.content: **S**ave **T**ake **M**ove **F**ree **R**eturn — The 5-step sequence: (1) Store node in temp, (2) Save data, (3) Move top/front pointer, (4) Free old node, (5) Return saved data.

mnemonics.title: Stack vs Queue Structure
mnemonics.content: **S**tack = **S**ingle pointer (**S**top/Head), **Q**ueue = **T**wo pointers (**F**ront + **R**ear) — Stack needs only one end; queue needs both ends for FIFO efficiency.

mnemonics.title: Array vs Linked List
mnemonics.content: **A**rray = **A**lways **F**ixed (size), **L**inked = **L**imited only by **M**emory — Arrays need predetermined size; linked lists grow/shrink dynamically using heap memory.

mnemonics.title: Memory Management Rules
mnemonics.content: **M**alloc **N**ULL **F**ree — **M**always check malloc return for **N**ULL, **N**ever dereference NULL pointers, **F**ree memory after pop/dequeue to prevent leaks.
